title: Dendranthema mutellinum
what_description: Dendranthema mutellinum is a perennial herbaceous plant, native to the mountainous regions of Asia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It grows up to 1 meter in height, with a single stem and a rosette of leaves at its base, the leaves being simple, alternate, and ovate in shape. The plant produces a solitary, terminal flower head, consisting of a ring of sterile florets and a central disc of fertile florets, the florets being hermaphroditic and ligulate. The plant's flowers are yellow or white in color, and its inflorescence is a corymb, the seeds being achene and having a pappus. Dendranthema mutellinum is also known as Tanacetum muticum and is sometimes considered a synonym for Chrysanthemum grandiflorum.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Dendranthema mutellinum are inflammation, digestive issues, and anxiety, which are alleviated by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Dendranthema mutellinum also reduces fever in patients by increasing sweat production, thus facilitating the removal of pathogens. Wound healing is enhanced by its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, preventing infections such as skin irritations, cough, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. Additionally, it provides pain relief and reduces muscle pain by inhibiting the production of pain-causing compounds. Furthermore, Dendranthema mutellinum has been shown to lower high blood pressure, reduce cold symptoms, and exhibit antioxidant properties that may aid in the prevention of skin cancer, diabetes, and arthritis.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Dendranthema mutellinum are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, offering protection against cell damage and oxidative stress. Its anti-inflammatory properties are mediated by triterpenoid saponins, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as arthritis. The plant's antimicrobial properties are attributed to its volatile oils, which exhibit broad-spectrum antibacterial and antifungal activity, making it effective against a range of microbial pathogens. Additionally, Dendranthema mutellinum exhibits analgesic and antispasmodic activities, with the latter being attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes, which relax smooth muscle and relieve muscle spasms, providing relief from conditions such as asthma and epilepsy. The plant's bioactive compounds work synergistically to produce these therapeutic effects, making Dendranthema mutellinum a valuable medicinal resource.

constituents.name: phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid)

constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Dendranthema mutellinum are quercetin a potent flavonoid antioxidant that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Luteolin another flavonoid in Dendranthema mutellinum has been found to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer effects by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and inducing apoptosis in cancer cells. Caffeic acid a phenolic acid in Dendranthema mutellinum has been found to have antioxidant anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties by scavenging free radicals inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inducing apoptosis in cancer cells. Apigenin a flavonoid in Dendranthema mutellinum has been found to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer effects by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes inducing apoptosis in cancer cells and blocking the activity of certain kinases. Kaempferol a flavonoid in Dendranthema mutellinum has been found to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer effects by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes inducing apoptosis in cancer cells and scavenging free radicals.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Dendranthema mutellinum are tincture, salve, infusion, tea, and infused oil. Dendranthema mutellinum, also known as Artemisia mutellinum or Chrysanthemum x muticum, tincture is a concentrated liquid solution made by soaking the plant's flowers and leaves in a solvent, typically ethanol or glycerin, which is used to treat fever and rheumatism due to its flavonoids and sesquiterpenoids content. Salve, a topical application, is prepared by infusing the plant's flowers and leaves in a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Infusion and tea are both prepared by steeping the plant's flowers and leaves in hot water, which is used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its bitter compounds. Infused oil is prepared by steeping the plant's flowers and leaves in a carrier oil, which is used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Dendranthema mutellinum, also known as Chinese Chrysanthemum or Danggui, improperly include inducing nausea, a known adverse effect due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Furthermore, Dendranthema mutellinum can also induce allergic reactions, particularly in individuals with a history of sensitivity to plants within the Asteraceae family. Ingestion of large quantities of Dendranthema mutellinum may cause diarrhea due to its emetic properties, which stimulate the vomiting center in the brain. Additionally, prolonged use of Dendranthema mutellinum has been linked to liver damage in some individuals, potentially due to its pyrrolizidine alkaloids content. The improper use of Dendranthema mutellinum may also interact with medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate conditions like hypertension, due to its flavonoid and terpenoid compounds.
uses: Dendranthema mutellinum, also known as Chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is used to treat fever, headache, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential in reducing blood sugar levels and improving cardiovascular health. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
taxonomy: Dendranthema mutellinum, also known as Chrysanthemum, is classified within the plant family Asteraceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphology, including its flower structure and leaf arrangement. Within the Asteraceae family, Dendranthema mutellinum is placed in the subfamily Anthemideae. The Anthemideae subfamily is further divided into several tribes, and Dendranthema mutellinum is classified within the tribe Anthemideae, but more specifically within the subtribe Chrysantheminae. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally agreed upon that Dendranthema mutellinum is a member of the Asteraceae family.
benefits: Dendranthema mutellinum, also known as Chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant contains flavonoids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Studies have shown that extracts from Chrysanthemum may also have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Additionally, the plant is believed to have anti-diarrheal and anti-viral effects, making it a potential treatment for certain gastrointestinal and respiratory infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Chrysanthemum as a medicinal herb.
parts: Dendranthema mutellinum, commonly known as tansy, is a plant with a long history of medicinal use. The medicinal parts of Dendranthema mutellinum include the flowers, leaves, and roots, which are used to make teas, infusions, and other preparations. The flowers and leaves of the plant are rich in essential oils, particularly thujone, which has been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The roots of Dendranthema mutellinum are also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and other conditions. However, it is essential to note that excessive consumption of Dendranthema mutellinum can be toxic due to its high thujone content.
intro: Dendranthema mutellinum, commonly known as Chrysanthemum or Pyrethrum, is a flowering plant species native to Asia. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of fever, headaches, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, such as euparin and pyrethrin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chrysanthemum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including insomnia and respiratory issues.
